raw_consumer_statement:
I am filing this complaint against Citibank for their failure to resolve a billing error for a purchase from XXXX in the amount of {$1800.00} ( Dispute # XXXX ). 
On XX/XX/year>, I purchased a table that arrived with a fundamental manufacturing mismatch ( incompatible parts ). On XX/XX/year>, the merchant authorized a return and arranged for their own carrier, XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX to pick up the item. 
The item was picked up on XX/XX/year>, and delivered to the merchants facility in XXXX, CA on XX/XX/year> ( XXXX XXXX # XXXX ). The shipment was signed for by " RECYCLED '' at the merchants location. 
Citibank has denied my dispute twice, stating that " the merchant will not honor the request. '' This is a violation of the Fair Credit Billing Act ( 15 U.S.C. 1666 ). Under the FCBA, a " billing error '' occurs when a creditor fails to reflect a credit for goods that were not accepted or were returned according to the merchants instructions.

Citibank is allowing the merchant to retain both the full payment of {$1800.00} and the physical merchandise. I have provided carrier-validated proof of delivery to the merchant, which the bank is choosing to ignore.

Resolution Requested : I request that Citibank immediately re-open this investigation, review the attached carrier proof of delivery, and make the {$1800.00} credit permanent.
